mnemonics: DAD CRAVES Protected Pies (Data-centric security, Access controls, DLP, Cryptography, Redundancy/Backups, Authentication, Version control, Encryption, Shredding, Patching, IAM, End-to-end encryption, Security groups)
keyPoints: Implement Data Loss Prevention (DLP) tools to monitor and control data transfer.
Use encryption for data at rest (e.g., in databases) and data in transit (e.g., over networks).
Employ strict Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies following the principle of least privilege.
commonMistakes: Assuming cloud provider handles all security, leading to misconfigured access controls.
Using weak or default encryption keys, making encrypted data easily compromised.
Neglecting to test data backup and disaster recovery procedures, assuming they will work.
